records.ety: [L. perpetratus, p. p. of perpetrare to effect, perpetrare; per + patrare to perform.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Perpetrated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Perpetrating.]

defs: To do or perform; to carry through; to execute, commonly in a bad sense; to commit (as a crime, an offense); to be guilty of; as, to perpetrate a foul deed.
quotes: What the worst perpetrate, or best endure.
